degraded: complexity classifier returned 'large' + touches_auth/rls/migrations — prose-keyword false positives (prior_source=keyword, history_n=0); the diff touches zero auth/RLS/migration code. Overridden to 'medium'; F11 recomputes risk from diff-predicates.

surface_verification.justification: Framework tooling + docs change: net-zero drift_parsers regex tolerance, a pure adopt Layers-inference helper, the model-parser (inferred) provenance downgrade, and spec-authoring docs. No runnable app surface (no web/cli/api entrypoint added or modified). The change is exercised entirely by the pytest suites (compliance 1060, adopt 398, shared drift 37, project/plan refs 14) plus the producer(adopt)->spec.md->model-parser round-trip probe recorded in the iterate ADR Confidence Calibration.

behaviors.behavior: shared parse_fr_table must not DROP an FR carrying a Layers column (4-col + 6-col), stays backward-compatible with 3/5-col
trailing-cell tolerance is linear-time (ReDoS-safe) on a pathological pipe-heavy row
adopt infers required_layers from surface: route/page=>e2e, migration/schema/table/RLS=>integration, always unit, unknown=>unit-only, multiple surfaces union
incidental 'table' in a UI component name must NOT infer integration (structured path-segment signal, not substring)
adopt renders the Layers column annotated (inferred); real brownfield fixture route FRs declare e2e end-to-end
model parser downgrades an (inferred)-marked Layers cell to advisory inferred_legacy (NOT explicit) so adopted FRs are not hard-gated (Spec 9 / R4)
behaviors.evidence: plugins/shipwright-compliance/tests/test_requirement_parse.py::test_adopt_inferred_marker_reads_as_advisory_not_explicit PASSED; producer->spec.md->parser round-trip probe in the ADR Confidence Calibration

behaviors.behavior: model parser reads an explicit integration Layers value + normalizes (case-fold/dedup/drop-unknown) + keeps legacy provenance distinct

MUST-FIX 1 (§11-R4 collapse): a headed Layers cell that is non-empty but resolves to zero valid canonical layers (author typo, e.g. 'int, db') stays explicit + is recorded in invalid_layers — NOT demoted to advisory legacy; empty cell still takes the legacy path
MUST-FIX 2: only the exact (inferred) marker downgrades to advisory; a post-rollout author writing (auto)/(adopted) stays explicit (hard-gated)

MUST-FIX 3: adopt's emitted (inferred) marker is bound to compliance's reader by a cross-plugin round-trip (render adopt spec -> parse via compliance -> inferred_legacy), so drift on either literal fails a test
MUST-FIX 4: a backend API route (fastapi/flask/express) does NOT infer e2e; a backend data route infers integration; e2e requires a UI framework or UI-file source
MUST-FIX 5: the drift_parsers trailing tolerance is linear-time on a NON-matching pathological row (no closing pipe, backtracking-on-failure path)
